title: Multitask Prompted Training Enables Zero-Shot Task Generalization

structured.summary: The paper investigates whether explicit multitask prompted training can achieve zero-shot task generalization in large language models. It demonstrates that this approach can outperform larger models on several tasks, utilizing a simple templating language to convert tasks into prompts and evaluate their performance.

strengths.point: Demonstrates strong zero-shot task generalization using explicit multitask training.
strengths.evidence: The model achieves improved performance over GPT-3 across 7/10 held-out datasets and 13/14 in the BIG-Bench benchmark (Results section).

strengths.point: Outperforms significantly larger models in several benchmarks.
strengths.evidence: Reported in the Results section that the proposed model often exceeds the performance of models 16× its size (Abstract).

strengths.point: Contributes to open research by releasing models and prompts.
strengths.evidence: All prompts and trained models are available on GitHub, enhancing reproducibility (Abstract).

weaknesses.evidence: The methodology is similar to existing works like Khashabi et al., 2020a and Ye et al., 2021 but does not adequately distinguish itself (Introduction, Related Work).

weaknesses.point: Insufficient details on templating language implementation.
weaknesses.evidence: The paper lacks technical details on the templating language, making it challenging to assess innovations (Section 4).

weaknesses.point: Limited evaluation variety may not represent full NLP task spectrum.
weaknesses.evidence: Evaluation is focused on BIG-Bench and a few tasks, potentially not covering a broad range of NLP tasks (Section 3).

weaknesses.point: Overemphasis on model size comparison without discussing computational efficiency.
weaknesses.evidence: While comparisons with larger models are made, the paper lacks a discussion of computational efficiency and resource requirements (Abstract, Results section).

weaknesses.point: Ambiguous zero-shot generalization definition with potential categorization bias.
weaknesses.evidence: Task categorization is described as fuzzy, but there is no clear statement on how this is addressed in the experimental setup (Section 3).
GPT-3 is used as the main comparison, but other state-of-the-art models are not considered (Results section).

weaknesses.point: Questionable generalization claim due to narrow task focus.
weaknesses.evidence: Limited to certain NLP tasks and BIG-Bench, lacking exploration of broader generalization across various task types (Results).

weaknesses.point: Potential bias in prompt collection from public contributors.
weaknesses.evidence: Methodology section lacks detail on the diversity of prompt collection, introducing possible bias.

weaknesses.point: Lack of detailed ablation studies to evaluate method components.
weaknesses.evidence: No comprehensive ablation studies are provided to understand the significance of various components (Results and Analysis).

weaknesses.point: Reproducibility concerns due to missing specifics in dataset splits and training configurations.
weaknesses.evidence: Details on dataset splits, model hyperparameters, and training configurations are not sufficiently documented (Conclusion, Abstract).

weaknesses.point: Handling of task categorization is unclear.
weaknesses.evidence: Section 3 mentions task categorization fuzziness but fails to describe how tasks were specifically assigned or grouped.

weaknesses.point: Scalability and real-world applicability of the method are not discussed.
weaknesses.evidence: The paper does not address scalability or potential real-world applications of the methodology.

questions.question: How does the templating language work and what are its specific advantages over existing methods?
questions.motivation: Understanding the contributions and potential innovation of the templating language is important to assess its impact.

questions.question: Why are other state-of-the-art models besides GPT-3 not considered for comparison?
questions.motivation: Inclusion of a broader range of baselines is crucial to validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

questions.question: How is task categorization handled to ensure unbiased zero-shot generalization evaluation?
questions.motivation: Clarification on this point is necessary to validate the experimental setup and ensure fair evaluation.
